title: HTTP Services, Clients, Boundaries, Security, and Graceful Shutdown · Runtime Contract: Http Client Lifecycle
summary: A small service accepts hostile input, calls a slow dependency, serves concurrent requests, and must shut down without losing work. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go http client lifecycle increment in a resilient secure HTTP service design. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reuse configured clients and transports, set request contexts and timeouts, close bodies, bound reads, and classify status separately from transport errors.
Design Handler and ServeMux boundaries, method and path semantics, request limits, response status, headers, and streaming order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Http Client Lifecycle · Theory Http Client Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
theory case — Service request 3 has a hostile label, oversized body, slow dependency, duplicate header, and client cancellation during shutdown. Bound resources, validate and encode by sink, preserve stable responses, and drain owned work without leaking internals.
